This ensures that Tailwind's JIT compiler correctly detects and generates the background, text, and border classes for the Hub cards in the production build. ## Type of Change - [x] Bug fix - [ ] New feature - [ ] Documentation update - [ ] Performance improvement - [ ] Other (describe) ## Related Issue Fixes #4084 ## Testing - [ ] Unit tests pass - [x] Tested Locally - [ ] New tests added ## Screenshots (MANDATORY for UI/UX changes) - [x] Attached Screenshots or Screen Recordings (showing before and after) ## Checklist - [x] Code follows project style guidelines - [x] Self-reviewed my code - [ ] Added comments where necessary - [ ] Updated documentation - [x] No new warnings generated ___ ## **CodeAnt-AI Description** **Keep Dashboard hub card colors visible in production builds** ### What Changed - Dashboard hub cards now use fixed, pre-defined color styles so their background, text, border, and hover colors still appear after deployment - Cards now stay on a safe default theme color if a hub color is not recognized, avoiding missing or broken styling ### Impact `✅ Correct Dashboard card colors after deploy` `✅ Fewer missing styles in production` `✅ Consistent Hub card hover states`

We check for security issues, mistakes in how you're setting up your infrastructure, and common code problems. We do this to make sure your changes are solid and won't cause any trouble later. ### Talking to CodeAnt AI Got a question or need a hand with something in your pull request? You can easily get in touch with CodeAnt AI right here. Just type the following in a comment on your pull request, and replace "Your question here" with whatever you want to ask: 
@codeant-ai ask: Your question here
This lets you have a chat with CodeAnt AI about your pull request, making it easier to understand and improve your code. #### Example 
@codeant-ai ask: Can you suggest a safer alternative to storing this secret?
### Preserve Org Learnings with CodeAnt You can record team preferences so CodeAnt AI applies them in future reviews. Reply directly to the specific CodeAnt AI suggestion (in the same thread) and replace "Your feedback here" with your input: 
@codeant-ai: Your feedback here
This helps CodeAnt AI learn and adapt to your team's coding style and standards. #### Example 
@codeant-ai: Do not flag unused imports.
### Retrigger review Ask CodeAnt AI to review the PR again, by typing: 
@codeant-ai: review

prs.title: fix: resolve replay system race condition
prs.body:
## Description

This PR resolves a race condition in the game replay/autoplay functionality where the playback would randomly stop before finishing the game. 

The issue was caused by a rigid `setInterval` timer that blindly triggered the next move every 1 second, regardless of whether the previous move's animation or engine evaluation had finished. When the board state fell behind the move list, the loop silently crashed.

## Type of Change

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to change)
- [ ] Documentation update

## Related Issue

Fixes #1799

prs.title: feat: integrate Cloudflare Turnstile CAPTCHA on membership form (#79)

labels.name: GSSoC'26
labels.description: Automatically applied to issues for GSSoC'26

prs.body:
### Description
This PR integrates the Cloudflare Turnstile CAPTCHA into the membership form to prevent spam submissions.

Closes #79

### ⚠️ Note for Maintainers: Backend Deployment
I have written and included the updated `Code.gs` logic in this PR, but since I don't have edit access to the live NexaSphere Google Sheet/Apps Script environment, I cannot deploy it to production myself.

**To get this live, whoever merges this will need to:**
1. Copy the updated `Code.gs` from this PR.
2. Open the Apps Script editor attached to the official Google Sheet and paste the new code.
3. Replace the `TURNSTILE_SECRET_KEY` variable in the script with the actual Secret Key from the Cloudflare dashboard.
4. Add `VITE_TURNSTILE_SITE_KEY` to the Vercel/production environment variables.
5. Hit "Deploy" in the Apps Script editor to make the new backend live.

P.S. - I apologise for the previous PR raised. It was closed because it did not have a 'Closes #ISSUE_NUMBER' line.  Please review this PR instead. Thank you!

prs.title: feat: integrate Cloudflare Turnstile CAPTCHA on membership form (#79)

labels.name: GSSoC'26
labels.description: Automatically applied to issues for GSSoC'26

labels.name: gssoc:no-linked-issue
labels.description: GSSoC: PR has no linked issue

prs.body:
### Description
This PR resolves issue #79 by integrating Cloudflare Turnstile CAPTCHA into the membership form to prevent spam submissions.

**Changes included:**
* Added the `@marsidev/react-turnstile` widget to the frontend, safely utilizing the `VITE_TURNSTILE_SITE_KEY` environment variable.
* Added server-side verification logic to `google-apps-script/Code.gs` to validate the CAPTCHA token before allowing the sheet to update.
* Synced with upstream `main` and resolved form validation merge conflicts.

---

### ⚠️ Note for Maintainers: Backend Deployment
I have written and included the updated `Code.gs` logic in this PR, but since I don't have edit access to the live NexaSphere Google Sheet/Apps Script environment, I cannot deploy it to production myself.

**To get this live, whoever merges this will need to:**
1. Copy the updated `Code.gs` from this PR.
2. Open the Apps Script editor attached to the official Google Sheet and paste the new code.
3. Replace the `TURNSTILE_SECRET_KEY` variable in the script with the actual Secret Key from the Cloudflare dashboard.
4. Add `VITE_TURNSTILE_SITE_KEY` to the Vercel/production environment variables.
5. Hit "Deploy" in the Apps Script editor to make the new backend live.

**Known UI Quirk:**
The custom red anti-gravity cursor occasionally gets stuck or overlapped by the system pointer when hovering directly over the Cloudflare iframe due to cross-origin security boundaries. I'm leaving this minor CSS/React refactor open for a future PR so we can get this critical security update merged now!

prs.title: feat: Add Google Calendar link button to upcoming events card (Fixes #2593)
prs.body:
## Proposed change

Resolves #2593

This PR implements the feature requested in issue #2593: adding a clickable calendar icon next to each event listed in the "Upcoming Events" section on the homepage.

When clicked, the icon generates a dynamic Google Calendar URL (with event name, date, and time correctly formatted) and opens it in a new tab.

### Key Changes:

* **Feature:** Added a new calendar icon (📅) link to each event card.
* **Code:** Implemented JavaScript logic in `frontend/src/app/page.tsx` to correctly format event dates into the required **ISO 8601 UTC format** for the Google Calendar API.
* **Fix:** Added `onClick={(e) => e.stopPropagation()}` to the link to ensure the event modal is not accidentally opened when the icon is clicked.

## Checklist

- [x] I've read and followed the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/OWASP/Nest/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.MD).
- [ ] I've run `make check-test` locally; all checks and tests passed.

issues.title: Custom Cursor shows even on default cursor mode
issues.body:
## Description

On opening the app, the custom cursor shows beneath the default cursor even when cursor is set to default.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Go to https://checkora.vercel.app/
2. See custom yellow circle cursor beneath the default mouse cursor

## Expected Behavior

The custom yellow circle cursor must be visible only upon selecting it from the cursor dropdown. It does not go till another mode is selected and then default mode is selected back

## Actual Behavior

The custom cursor shows on start without actually selecting it.

issues.title: Light/Dark theme toggle does not work
issues.body:
## Description

The light theme toggle does not work as intended. The theme won't switch to light mode when pressing the button.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Go to https://checkora.vercel.app/
2. Click on the theme button
3. See that the theme does not switch

## Expected Behavior

The dark mode should change to light mode.

## Actual Behavior

The dark mode does not change to light mode.

issues.title: [Feature] Improve Light Theme Contrast and Accessibility
issues.body:
## Problem Statement

The current light theme suffers from readability and contrast issues, particularly when compared to the highly polished dark theme. The light gold accent text (such as the "Hybrid Intelligence" heading) washes out against the cream/beige checkerboard background, making it difficult to read. Furthermore, the yellow primary action buttons ("Start Game" and "Lessons") lose their visual impact, and the solid grey navigation bar feels disconnected from the warm, airy tones of the light mode.

## Proposed Solution

Introduce a dedicated, slightly darker accent color palette exclusively for the light theme to improve accessibility and aesthetic cohesion. Specifically:

Accent Text: Shift the gold text to a darker, richer gold, or a contrasting color like deep navy blue or charcoal.

Buttons: Add a subtle drop shadow or a slightly darker border to the buttons to help them stand out from the bright background.

Navigation Bar: Lighten the navbar to a translucent white or a softer, warmer off-white so it blends more naturally with the rest of the page.

I'd like to work on this under GSSoC 2026, please assign it to me!

issues.title: Replay system stops early — does not play through all moves
issues.body:
## Description
When using the in-game replay controls after the match ends, the replay stops after a few moves and does not complete the full game.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Complete a game (PvP or vs AI)
2. Click on analyse game
3. Observe that the replay stops stepping through moves before the game is finished

## Expected Behavior
The replay should step through every move in the game from start to finish.

## Actual Behavior
The replay stops early, only showing the first few moves. The browser console shows `Replay move added:` logs stopping partway through the move list.

issues.title: [BUG]: Game glitches out when playing against AI
issues.body:
## Description

When playing against AI, multiple bugs occur:
1. When the AI checks the user's King, the red check box is highlighted on the AI's King instead of the user's.
2. The entire system then freezes, with the "AI is thinking state".
3. The game only resumes when the user pauses and unpauses the game.
4. Upon unpausing, the user's pieces (immediately the next move after the AI checked the user) move on their own by the AI, and it's the AI's turn again.
5. The victory banner after the match ends is shown for the user no matter who wins.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Go to [the checkora site](https://checkora.vercel.app/)
2. Start a new match against AI (pick any colour it doesn't matter)
3. Play out the game
4. You'll find all the mentioned bugs above

## Expected Behavior

Accurate Check Highlighting: When the user is placed in check, the red warning highlight should appear on the user's King, not the AI's.

Continuous Game Flow: After the AI completes its move, the game state should transition smoothly back to the user's turn without freezing or requiring manual intervention (like pausing/unpausing).

Exclusive Player Control: The AI should only move its own pieces. It should never automatically execute moves on behalf of the user.

Accurate Match Results: The post-game banner should correctly reflect the actual outcome of the match (e.g., displaying a "Defeat" screen if the AI wins).

issues.title: Wrong King gets check status
issues.body: ## Description When playing against the AI, if the user is put in check, the AI incorrectly applies the check status to its own King instead of the user's. Furthermore, the AI becomes stuck in a permanent "AI is thinking..." state and fails to make a move. During this state, the game timer continues to run, and the player is restricted to making only pre-moves. This deadlock can only be resolved by the player pausing and unpausing the game. Additionally, upon unpausing, the AI moves on behalf of the player. ## Steps to Reproduce Start a game against the AI. Allow the AI to make a move that puts the user in check. Observe that the game incorrectly highlights or flags the AI's King as being in check. Observe the AI entering a permanent "AI is thinking..." loop. Attempt to play during this state to confirm only pre-moves are allowed. Pause and unpause the game to observe the resolution of the deadlock. ## Expected Behavior The AI should correctly identify that the user is in check. The AI should proceed to make its next move without entering a "thinking" deadlock, and the game clock/mechanics should function normally. ## Actual Behavior The game incorrectly flags the AI's King as being in check when it should flag the user's. The AI engine subsequently hangs, failing to complete its turn, and enters an infinite "AI is thinking..." state. The game remains unresponsive to standard moves until the player manually triggers a state update by pausing and unpausing the game, upon which the player's pieces are moved automatically by the AI ## Screenshots https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/51038052-c623-4a42-b2e8-5aecf9f3b079 https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f3732625-a46f-49cd-8bb2-d0f7ebc37742

issues.title: [Feature]: Add 7-Day Expiring Match History Replay System
issues.body:
## Problem Statement

Currently, users cannot review their past games or download PGNs after a match ends. However, permanently storing every game for every user would quickly bloat the database and increase storage costs for the project.

## Proposed Solution

I propose adding an account-specific "Match History" tab with a 7-day automatic data retention policy, alongside an interactive match replay viewer, and an option to download the match history PGN. This allows players to deeply analyse their recent games while keeping server storage minimal.

## Alternatives Considered

Even though we have the option to save the match PGN data directly on the user's local storage, it is available only immediately after the match ends and not after. Additionally, cross-device sync breaks completely. If a user plays a game on their laptop, they won't see that game's history when they log in from their phone.

issues.title: Bug: Post-game summary modal persistently renders when navigating to the Dashboard
issues.body:
## Description

When navigating to the Dashboard via the user dropdown menu, the post-game summary modal from the most recently completed game automatically pops up.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Complete a game (e.g., against the AI).

2. Dismiss the post-game summary or navigate to home.

3. Open the user dropdown menu and click Dashboard.

4. Observe that the post-game summary modal reappears instead of showing a clean dashboard.

## Expected Behavior

Clicking "Dashboard" should route the user to a clean dashboard view. The post-game summary modal should only appear immediately after a game concludes and should permanently dismiss once closed or navigated away from.

## Actual Behavior

The app seems to be holding onto the previous game's state. Loading the dashboard triggers the post-game summary modal to reappear every time.

## Environment

- OS: macOS
- Browser: Brave

issues.title: Bug: Dropdown menu disappears before options can be clicked (Hover Gap Issue)
issues.body:
Description:
When hovering over the user profile button in the navigation bar, the dropdown menu appears as expected. However, when moving the cursor down to select an option, the menu immediately disappears, making it impossible to click "Dashboard", "Stats", "Delete Account", or "Logout".

Steps to Reproduce:

Go to the main navigation bar.

Hover the mouse cursor over the user profile/username button.

Observe that the dropdown menu appears.

Attempt to move the mouse cursor into the dropdown menu to click an option.

Observe that the menu disappears before the cursor can reach the items.

Expected Behavior:
The dropdown menu should remain visible and active as long as the user's cursor is hovering over the trigger button or anywhere within the dropdown menu itself.

Actual Behavior:
The menu closes as soon as the cursor leaves the trigger button. There appears to be a gap causing a mouseleave event to fire before the cursor registers as being over the dropdown container.

Possible Cause:
This looks like a standard CSS hover gap issue. Ensuring the dropdown container sits flush against the trigger element (or adding an invisible padding/bridge between them) should keep the hover state active.

issues.title: [Feature Request] Add Spam Protection (reCAPTCHA / Turnstile) to Public Forms

labels.name: GSSoC'26
labels.description: Automatically applied to issues for GSSoC'26

labels.name: NSOC'26
labels.description: Automatically applied to newly opened issues for NSOC'26

issues.body:
**Description**:
Currently, the public-facing forms (Membership, Recruitment, Core Team Applications, and Contact) do not have any automated bot protection. As the platform scales, this leaves the PostgreSQL database highly vulnerable to automated spam submissions. This can clutter the Admin Dashboard with fake data and unnecessarily drain backend resources.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Implement a CAPTCHA solution to verify human users before form submission. Cloudflare Turnstile (which is highly privacy-focused and low-friction) or Google reCAPTCHA v3 are ideal candidates since they run silently in the background without frustrating the user.

The implementation would require two parts:

Frontend (src): Integrate the chosen CAPTCHA provider to generate a token when the user submits a public form.

Backend (server-java or server-python): Create a middleware or validation step to verify the token with the provider's API before executing the database insertion.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

Honeypot fields: Adding invisible input fields that only bots will fill out. This is easier to implement but less secure against advanced bots than a dedicated CAPTCHA service.

reCAPTCHA v2 (checkbox): Effective, but disrupts the clean UI/UX of the current forms compared to invisible alternatives like v3 or Turnstile.

@Ayushh-Sharmaa I'd like to work on this feature under GSSoC 2026, could you please assign the issue to me?

issues.title: [BUG]: Resign button does not work.
issues.body:
## Description

When trying to resign against the opponent, the resign button does not work.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Go to 'https://checkora.vercel.app/play/'
2. Start a new game.
3. Play a few moves/click resign button directly.

## Expected Behavior

Upon pressing the resign button, a victory/loss page is supposed to pop up showing which player won.

## Actual Behavior

The victory page never pops up. The game continues like before.

issues.title: [BUG] Playing as black against AI labels the player as AI (You) and gives the player's name to the AI.
issues.body: After choosing to play against AI as black, the player is incorrectly labeled as "AI" in the player card, showing as AI (YOU), and the player's name is displayed next to the AI (playing white). Steps to reproduce: 1. Open the vercel app: https://checkora.vercel.app/play/ (or you can run it locally too) 2. Choose to play against AI. 3. Enter your username (xyz). 4. Choose to play black. 5. Start game. Expected Behaviour: 1. The player card should display a player's username (like "Guest" or "xyz"), not "AI (YOU)". And for the AI it should show "AI (White)"

issues.title: Game freezes completely on changing/switching tabs.
issues.body:
Description:
The game timer freezes when tabs are changed, and subsequently, the game freezes completely.

Steps to Reproduce:

Open Checkora and start a new match.

Click on a piece to make your first move, the 10-minute timer will start automatically

Open a new browser tab or minimize the browser window, leaving the Checkora tab in the background.

Switch back to the Checkora tab.

See error: The timer has frozen, and the game board becomes unresponsive to any clicks and moves.

Expected Behavior
The timer should keep running even when the game tab is inactive. Upon returning to the tab, the timer should continue showing the correct remaining time, and the player should be able to resume making moves.

Actual Behavior
The timer pauses while the tab is inactive, causing the visual clock to fall out of sync with the actual time passed. Moreover, returning to the active tab causes the game state to freeze entirely, preventing any further interactions with the chessboard.

Screenshots
N/A - A video would better demonstrate this, but the visual state simply shows the timer stuck at the moment the tab was backgrounded.

Additional Context
This is likely caused by the browser's native resource-saving features, which heavily throttle setInterval, setTimeout, and requestAnimationFrame in background tabs.

To fix the timer desync, the frontend countdown logic likely needs to be refactored to calculate the time delta using Date.now() or performance.now() against a saved start-time variable, rather than relying on a strictly ticking interval counter. The complete board freeze might be a side effect of the frontend timer state falling out of sync with the backend game state.
